linting.notes: Ruff is configured in pyproject.toml with target-version py310, line-length 100. Selected rules: E, W, F, I, B, C4, UP, ARG, SIM. Ignores E501 (line too long) and some bugbear rules.

testing.mock_patterns: Uses pytest fixtures for mocking Kubernetes clients and API responses
testing.notes: Tests are organized by domain (composites/, domains/, training/). The evals/ directory contains evaluation tests marked with @pytest.mark.eval that require LLM providers and are ignored in CI. asyncio_mode is set to 'auto' in pyproject.toml. 520 unit/integration tests passed in 6.74s with 48% coverage.

ci.notes: CI runs on pull_request and push to main. Tests run on Python 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12 in a matrix. The ci-status job aggregates all check results and fails if any job fails. Container build workflow builds and publishes container images. Eval workflow runs LLM-based evaluation tests (not gating).

build.notes: Project uses uv for dependency management (modern, fast pip replacement). No build step required for Python source. Container images built with Containerfile using Red Hat UBI 9 Python 3.12 base image. Makefile provides convenience targets for development and container management.

conventions.test_naming: Test functions use test_* prefix. Organized by domain: composites/, domains/, training/, integration/, utils/. Async tests use pytest-asyncio.
conventions.import_style: Absolute imports from src/rhoai_mcp. First-party imports known via ruff isort config. Type hints required (mypy disallow_untyped_defs=true).
conventions.notes: Project structure follows domain-driven design with composites/ (higher-level tools), domains/ (resource-specific logic), and clients/ (K8s API abstraction). Code requires Python 3.10+ and uses modern type hints with Pydantic models.

test_commands.notes: Passed: Success: no issues found in 95 source files

container_recipe.notes: uv must be installed from astral.sh script and added to PATH. All commands validated successfully in Python 3.12 container. No external dependencies (databases, clusters) required for unit/integration tests.
